blurb: Explosively crisp, honey-sweet apple — the Front Range's most dependable tree fruit, hardy and late-blooming.
water: Moderate, regular — deep weekly soak in summer
spread: Clumping — single tree; needs a pollinizer apple nearby
seasons: Pink-white blossom mid–late spring; large red-blushed fruit Sept–Oct
wildlife: Blossoms feed bees; dropped fruit feeds birds & mammals
toxic: Fruit flesh edible; seeds contain cyanogenic amygdalin (mildly toxic if chewed in quantity)
